description: Update the shared relational API metadata formatter so `_etag` derivation recursively removes every server-generated field named `id`, `link`, `_etag`, or `_lastModifiedDate` before canonical JSON serialization. Acceptance: relational resources and descriptors use the same canonicalization path; `link` subtrees at root, reference, collection, nested collection, and `_ext` locations never affect the hash; real resource-state value changes still produce a different hash; existing metadata injection behavior continues to work. Note: any readable-profile `RefreshEtag` behavior from the original implementation is superseded by the later profile-invariant ETag tasks.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/RelationalApiMetadataFormatterTests.cs` to confirm coverage for nested `link`, root metadata, descriptor bodies, and a real reference identity value change.

description: Add a typed `If-Match`/write-precondition contract and use it instead of direct backend header reads. Acceptance: Core constructs the contract once from request headers using exact opaque-string semantics; absent means the header key is not present after frontend filtering; any non-dropped blank/whitespace value is treated as present and mismatched; no quote normalization, entity-tag list parsing, splitting, trimming, or malformed-header rejection is performed; relational and descriptor backend code consume only the typed contract, not `Headers["If-Match"]`.
steps-to-verify: Review the added Core and backend precondition tests to confirm coverage for absent, exact, quoted, whitespace, and collapsed multi-value `If-Match` inputs.

Run: `rg "Headers\\[[^]]*\\\"If-Match\\\"|TryGetValue\\(\\\"If-Match\\\"|ContainsKey\\(\\\"If-Match\\\"|Contains\\(\\\"If-Match\\\"" src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core -g '*.cs'` and verify only the Core precondition factory reads the raw header in production code.

description: Add `UpsertResult.UpsertFailureETagMisMatch` and wire all HTTP mappings to the existing 412 optimistic-lock response shape. Acceptance: POST upsert-as-update and POST create-new-with-If-Match can return the new result; `UpsertHandler` maps it to the same `urn:ed-fi:api:optimistic-lock-failed` body used by `UpdateFailureETagMisMatch` and `DeleteFailureETagMisMatch`; retry policy does not treat ETag mismatch as retryable; existing update/delete 412 behavior remains unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/Handler/UpsertHandlerTests.cs` and `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/Handler/DeadlockRetryPolicyTests.cs` to confirm `UpsertFailureETagMisMatch` maps to the standard 412 response body and is not treated as retryable.

description: Historical DMS-1005 implementation task that plumbed a readable profile ETag comparison surface through Core/backend relational write contracts. This profile-projected ETag surface is superseded by the later profile-invariant ETag tasks; do not use this completed task as current acceptance criteria. Current behavior must compare and return full-resource ETags for profiled reads and writes.
steps-to-verify: Review the later profile-invariant ETag tasks before changing this area; their acceptance criteria replace the original profile-projected expectations.
Review updated Core/backend tests to confirm no active test still requires profile-projected ETags for reads, writes, or `If-Match`.

description: Implement a relational current-representation precondition helper for existing relational-table resources. Acceptance: inside the write transaction, the helper re-resolves or confirms the target, locks the target `dms.Document` row (`FOR UPDATE` for PostgreSQL, `UPDLOCK, HOLDLOCK, ROWLOCK` for SQL Server), hydrates/materializes the current full-resource representation, computes the link-excluding `_etag`, and compares it exactly to the typed precondition value while holding the row lock through subsequent DML or delete. Note: the original profile-projected comparison expectation is superseded by the later `Relational Full-Resource If-Match` task.
steps-to-verify: Review the added backend unit tests to confirm PostgreSQL and SQL Server lock SQL and exact full-resource comparison success/failure are covered.

description: Enforce optional `If-Match` for relational-table PUT and POST upsert flows in the authoritative in-transaction path. Acceptance: absent `If-Match` preserves existing behavior; exact match allows changed writes and guarded no-op success; mismatch returns `UpdateFailureETagMisMatch` or `UpsertFailureETagMisMatch` before reference resolution, profile creatability validation, merge validation, or DML after target existence and authorization are satisfied; authorization failures still win over `412`; POST resolving to create-new with present `If-Match` returns `UpsertFailureETagMisMatch` once target identity resolution proves the request is an insert, before deeper backend validation, while earlier malformed-request/profile-usage failures still win; PUT missing target remains 404.
steps-to-verify: Review the added executor/repository unit tests to confirm absent-header behavior, exact match, mismatch short-circuit after authorization but before deeper backend validation, authorization-failure precedence, PUT missing-target 404, POST-as-update mismatch, and POST create-new-with-If-Match are all covered.

description: Enforce optional `If-Match` for relational-table DELETE by id. Acceptance: missing target still returns 404; authorization failures still win over `412`; absent `If-Match` preserves existing delete behavior; present exact match locks and compares the current full-resource representation before deleting; present mismatch returns `DeleteFailureETagMisMatch` without issuing the delete; the lock is held through delete/commit; existing FK conflict mapping to 409 remains unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Review the added delete-path unit tests to confirm PostgreSQL and SQL Server lock SQL, exact success, mismatch 412 result, 404 precedence, authorization precedence, and FK conflict preservation are all covered.

description: Change stale guarded no-op handling to honor present `If-Match`. Acceptance: when the shared guarded no-op freshness recheck reports stale and the precondition is present, PUT returns `UpdateFailureETagMisMatch` and POST-as-update returns `UpsertFailureETagMisMatch` immediately with no stale retry; when the precondition is absent, the existing single stale-no-op retry remains unchanged; behavior applies to both no-profile and profiled guarded no-op paths.
steps-to-verify: Review the added stale guarded-no-op unit tests to confirm `DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor` and repository retry behavior differ correctly when a precondition is present versus absent.

description: Refactor descriptor POST and PUT onto a shared descriptor current-state/precondition helper so they enforce the same typed optional `If-Match` semantics as relational-table resources. Acceptance: `DescriptorWriteRequest` carries the typed precondition; descriptor POST resolving to create-new with present `If-Match` returns `UpsertFailureETagMisMatch`; descriptor POST-as-update and PUT use the shared helper to lock the owning `dms.Document` row, materialize the current full descriptor resource-state representation, compute the exact current full-resource ETag, and compare it exactly; existing-target mismatch short-circuits after existence and authorization are satisfied but before deeper validation or DML; absent `If-Match` preserves existing behavior; profile media-type headers do not create a descriptor-specific projected ETag surface; unsupported descriptor profile writes still fail before persistence/precondition code if they are unsupported; if the shared current-state comparison makes descriptor POST-as-update unchanged detection trivial while touching this code, reuse it to short-circuit that overlap without extra stamp/journal work, but leave broader descriptor stamp/journal hardening in `DMS-1008`; identity/URI immutability behavior remains unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/DescriptorWriteHandlerDeleteTests.cs`, `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/DescriptorWriteHandlerNoOpTests.cs`, and any added descriptor write tests to confirm absent precondition, exact full-resource match, mismatch short-circuit after authorization but before deeper validation, POST create-new-with-If-Match, POST-as-update exact/mismatch and unchanged-existing overlap short-circuit, immutable URI changes, profile headers do not change the comparison surface, and unsupported profiled descriptor writes are all covered.

description: Enforce optional `If-Match` for descriptor DELETE. Acceptance: descriptor delete carries a typed precondition rather than raw headers and reuses the shared descriptor current-state/precondition helper instead of duplicating lock/compare logic; missing descriptor target still returns 404; authorization failures still win over `412`; DELETE compares against the full descriptor-resource ETag and ignores profile media-type headers for precondition purposes; absent `If-Match` preserves existing descriptor delete behavior; exact match locks the owning `dms.Document` row, materializes the current descriptor representation, and allows delete; mismatch returns `DeleteFailureETagMisMatch` without issuing delete; descriptor FK conflict mapping remains unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/DescriptorWriteHandlerDeleteTests.cs` and any added delete-path tests to confirm absent precondition, exact match, mismatch, missing-target precedence, authorization precedence, DELETE full-resource ETag behavior, and FK conflict preservation are all covered.

description: Add coverage proving resource links do not affect `If-Match`. Acceptance: a client ETag obtained with `DataManagement:ResourceLinks:Enabled=true` succeeds when the write precondition check runs against a link-stripped representation; a client ETag obtained with links disabled succeeds when the backend has a link-bearing intermediate document; flag-on and flag-off responses for the same resource-state surface produce identical `_etag` values; any failures are fixed in the shared canonicalization or response strip path, not by special-casing `If-Match`.
steps-to-verify: Review the added unit and integration/E2E tests to confirm link-bearing versus link-stripped canonicalization and both `DataManagement:ResourceLinks:Enabled` flag states are covered without special-casing `If-Match`.

description: Add cross-dialect relational integration tests proving dependency identity changes make stale `If-Match` fail. Acceptance: using the small referential-identity fixture, create Student and dependent ResourceA, capture ResourceA `_etag`, update Student.studentUniqueId with `allowIdentityUpdates=true`, verify ResourceA's served `_etag` changed due to propagated reference identity values, then assert a stale `If-Match` on ResourceA PUT returns 412 for both PostgreSQL and SQL Server.
steps-to-verify: Review the added PostgreSQL and SQL Server integration tests to confirm they use Student -> ResourceA from the small referential-identity fixtures and assert both ETag change and stale `If-Match` 412 behavior.

description: Move or retag the existing resource `If-Match` E2E scenarios so they execute against the relational backend without reducing scenario coverage. Acceptance: the scenarios in `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Resources/etag.feature` still cover retrieving ETag, PUT exact match, request-body `_etag` ignored, PUT mismatch 412, DELETE mismatch 412, and DELETE exact match; the relational backend is enabled for this lane; setup/teardown uses the DMS E2E local stack instructions.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Resources/etag.feature` and its generated test metadata to confirm the feature runs in the relational lane (`@relational-backend` tagging or equivalent lane configuration) and still covers the same six scenarios.

description: Change relational and descriptor readable-profile reads so profile projection filters the response body but preserves the full-resource `_etag` already materialized before projection. Acceptance: profiled GET and query responses for relational resources and descriptors keep the same `_etag` value as an unprofiled response for the same document and `ContentVersion`; `_lastModifiedDate` remains preserved; `IReadableProfileProjector` continues to preserve root server metadata (`id`, `_etag`, `_lastModifiedDate`) while filtering non-metadata fields; profile-hidden fields are absent from the body but still covered by the returned `_etag`; relational and descriptor read handlers do not recompute `_etag` from the profile-projected JSON surface after calling `IReadableProfileProjector.Project`.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.cs` and `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/DescriptorReadHandler.cs` to confirm readable-profile projection no longer calls `RelationalApiMetadataFormatter.RefreshEtag`.
Review `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/Profile/ReadableProfileProjectorTests.cs` to confirm readable projection still preserves root metadata fields without recomputing them.

Run: `rg "RefreshEtag\(" src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.cs src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/DescriptorReadHandler.cs` and verify it prints no read-handler call after readable-profile projection; remaining formatter utility or write-response calls are handled by later tasks.

description: Remove the readable-profile ETag comparison surface from the typed write-precondition contract. Acceptance: `WritePrecondition` carries only whether a precondition is absent or the exact opaque `If-Match` value; `ReadableEtagProjectionContext`, `EtagProjectionContext`, and the obsolete `ReadableEtagProjectionContext.cs` file are removed from production code; Core `UpsertHandler` and `UpdateByIdHandler` construct write preconditions from request headers without deriving readable-profile projection context; DELETE behavior remains unchanged and still does not attach profile ETag context; relational write contracts, executor request comments, and test doubles no longer describe an authoritative profiled-write ETag surface; exact opaque-string semantics from the original DMS-1005 work remain covered.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/WritePrecondition.cs`, `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Backend/WritePreconditionFactory.cs`, `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/Utility.cs`, `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/UpsertHandler.cs`, `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/UpdateByIdHandler.cs`, and `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalWriteContracts.cs` to confirm profile ETag context plumbing and stale profiled-Etag comments are gone and the precondition factory still treats raw header values opaquely.

Run: `rg "ReadableEtagProjectionContext|EtagProjectionContext|CreateReadableEtagProjectionContext" src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External -g '*.cs'` and verify there are no production references.

description: Update relational `If-Match` checking to compare against the current full-resource ETag regardless of active profile headers. Acceptance: `RelationalCurrentEtagPreconditionChecker` locks the target row, hydrates/materializes the current external full resource representation with descriptor projection still enabled where the read plan needs descriptor URI hydration, computes the link-excluding full-resource `_etag`, and performs exact opaque-string comparison without applying readable-profile projection; the checker no longer depends on `IReadableProfileProjector`; shared, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server service-construction tests are updated for the constructor change; a client ETag obtained from a profiled GET succeeds when the full resource has not changed; the same ETag fails after a change to any profile-hidden persisted field because the full-resource validator changed.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalCurrentEtagPreconditionChecker.cs` to confirm it no longer depends on `IReadableProfileProjector` or branches on profile ETag context.

Review unit tests in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/RelationalCurrentEtagPreconditionCheckerTests.cs` to confirm projected-surface expectations were replaced with full-resource comparison expectations, including a hidden-field-change mismatch case.

Review `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/ReferenceResolverServiceCollectionExtensionsTests.cs`, `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/PostgresqlReferenceResolverServiceCollectionExtensionsTests.cs`, and `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/MssqlReferenceResolverServiceCollectionExtensionsTests.cs` to confirm `IRelationalCurrentEtagPreconditionChecker` still resolves after the constructor change.

description: Return full-resource ETags from successful relational writes, including profiled PUT, profiled POST-as-update, inserts, changed writes, and guarded no-op successes. Acceptance: `RelationalCommittedRepresentationReader` reads the committed full external response and returns the existing full-resource `_etag` without applying readable-profile projection or recomputing from a projected body; the reader no longer depends on `IReadableProfileProjector`; shared, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server service-construction tests are updated for the constructor change; success ETag headers for profiled writes match a follow-up unprofiled GET; no-profile write behavior remains unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalCommittedRepresentationReader.cs` and `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor.cs` to confirm write success ETag extraction uses the full committed response.

Review `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/RelationalCommittedRepresentationReaderTests.cs` and write executor/repository tests to confirm profiled write success ETags are asserted equal to the full-resource ETag rather than a projected ETag.

Review `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/ReferenceResolverServiceCollectionExtensionsTests.cs`, `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/PostgresqlReferenceResolverServiceCollectionExtensionsTests.cs`, and `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/MssqlReferenceResolverServiceCollectionExtensionsTests.cs` to confirm `IRelationalCommittedRepresentationReader` still resolves after the constructor change.

description: Align descriptor read, write, and delete ETag behavior with profile-invariant full-resource semantics. Acceptance: profiled descriptor GET/query responses preserve the descriptor full-resource `_etag`; descriptor POST/PUT no-op and changed-write success ETag headers are computed from the full descriptor state; descriptor POST-as-update, PUT, and DELETE `If-Match` checks compare against the same full descriptor ETag; profile media-type headers do not create a descriptor-specific projected ETag surface; unsupported descriptor profile writes, if any, still fail before persistence/precondition code; PostgreSQL, SQL Server, and existing descriptor E2E read-profile expectations are updated away from profile-projected descriptor ETags.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/DescriptorReadHandler.cs` and `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/DescriptorWriteHandler.cs` to confirm descriptor ETag comparison and returned ETags use one full-resource surface.
Review descriptor read/write tests to confirm profiled descriptor reads preserve the full ETag and descriptor write/delete `If-Match` scenarios use that same ETag.

Review `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Descriptors/ReadDescriptorsValidation.feature` and its generated feature code, if committed, to confirm profiled descriptor reads assert equality with the unprofiled descriptor `_etag` rather than inequality.

description: Rewrite unit tests that currently encode profile-projected ETag behavior so they assert profile-invariant full-resource validators. Acceptance: tests that previously expected projected `_etag` values are changed to assert projected response bodies preserve the original full-resource `_etag`; tests explicitly cover relational GET, relational query, descriptor GET, descriptor query, relational current `If-Match`, committed write response ETags, Core write-precondition construction, delete precondition behavior, metadata formatter helper naming, and shared/PostgreSQL/SQL Server service registration after constructor cleanup; stale tests and helpers named around `ReadableEtagProjectionContext` or profile-projected ETags are removed or renamed.
steps-to-verify: Run: `rg "profile-projected|projected ETag|projected_etag|ReadableEtagProjectionContext|EtagProjectionContext|refreshes_etag|recomputes.*etag" src/dms -g '*Tests*.cs'` and verify remaining matches, if any, refer to the new invariant behavior or non-production historical comments only.

description: Add integration coverage proving full-resource ETags remain stable across profile projection but still change for hidden persisted-state changes. Acceptance: for at least one relational resource with a readable/writable profile, an unprofiled GET and profiled GET for the same document return the same `_etag`; a profiled `If-Match` write succeeds when the full resource has not changed; updating a profile-hidden persisted field changes the profiled response `_etag`; a subsequent write using the stale profiled ETag returns 412; the hidden-field assertion must be caused by a persisted resource-state change, not by link injection, profile response shape, or metadata-only changes; the same behavioral coverage exists for both PostgreSQL and SQL Server profile integration fixtures; descriptor profile read coverage, where supported by the test fixture, proves descriptor profiled GET/query preserve the same full descriptor ETag.
steps-to-verify: Review PostgreSQL and SQL Server integration tests to confirm they exercise profiled GET/query ETag invariance, profiled `If-Match` success, hidden persisted-field ETag change, and stale profiled ETag 412 behavior. Prefer an existing profile fixture with a non-identity hidden persisted member so the regression does not depend on response-shape-only differences.

description: Add or update DMS E2E profile scenarios so the API-level behavior matches legacy ODS-compatible full-resource ETags. Acceptance: through the running DMS API, a profiled GET and unprofiled GET for the same relational resource return the same `_etag` in the response body; existing descriptor readable-profile E2E coverage also asserts the profiled descriptor `_etag` equals the unprofiled descriptor `_etag`; a profiled PUT/POST using that value in the `If-Match` header succeeds when the full resource has not changed and returns an `etag` response header matching a follow-up unprofiled GET; a stale profiled ETag returns the existing optimistic-lock 412 response after a profile-hidden persisted field changes, for example a School member such as `shortNameOfInstitution` hidden by `E2E-Test-School-IncludeOnly`; profile filtering of response body fields remains unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Profiles` and `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Descriptors/ReadDescriptorsValidation.feature` to confirm the profile ETag scenarios run through normal profile header/claim-set setup rather than backend-only test doubles, and update generated `.feature.cs` files if this repository still commits them.

description: Run the final formatting and stale-reference sweep after the profile-invariant ETag implementation lands. Acceptance: modified C# files are formatted with CSharpier; production code has no remaining `ReadableEtagProjectionContext`, `EtagProjectionContext`, or profile-projected ETag logic; tests, E2E feature files, generated feature code, and active design docs no longer assert profile-projected ETags except explicitly superseded historical notes; targeted Core, backend unit, PostgreSQL integration, SQL Server integration, and E2E profile/ETag suites pass or any environment-only failures are documented.
steps-to-verify: Run: `rg "ReadableEtagProjectionContext|EtagProjectionContext|profile-projected|projected.*_etag|RefreshEtag\(" src/dms -g '*.cs' -g '*.feature'` and verify every remaining match is either unrelated readable profile projection, canonical metadata formatting, or intentional historical/task text.

Run: `rg "recompute.*_etag|recomputes.*_etag|profile-projected.*_etag|profile.*projected.*ETag|ReadableEtagProjectionContext|EtagProjectionContext" reference/design/backend-redesign src/dms -g '*.md' -g '*.cs' -g '*.feature'` and verify every remaining match is either an explicitly superseded historical note or unrelated profile projection wording.
Run the targeted test commands from the preceding profile-invariant ETag tasks and document any skipped integration/E2E runs with the concrete environment reason.
